metadata.summary: A web server for spawning and communicating with Jupyter kernels

documentation:
# Jupyter Kernel Gateway

[![Google Group](https://img.shields.io/badge/-Google%20Group-lightgrey.svg)](https://groups.google.com/forum/#!forum/jupyter)
[![PyPI version](https://badge.fury.io/py/jupyter_kernel_gateway.svg)](https://badge.fury.io/py/jupyter_kernel_gateway)
[![Build Status](https://github.com/jupyter/kernel_gateway/workflows/Tests/badge.svg)](https://github.com/jupyter/kernel_gateway/actions?query=workflow%3ATests)
[![Documentation Status](http://readthedocs.org/projects/jupyter-kernel-gateway/badge/?version=latest)](https://jupyter-kernel-gateway.readthedocs.io/en/latest/?badge=latest)

## Overview

Jupyter Kernel Gateway is a web server that provides headless access to
Jupyter kernels. Your application communicates with the kernels remotely,
through REST calls and Websockets rather than ZeroMQ messages.
There are no provisions for editing notebooks through the Kernel Gateway.
The following operation modes, called personalities, are supported
out of the box:

- Send code snippets for execution using the
  [Jupyter kernel protocol](https://jupyter-client.readthedocs.io/en/latest/messaging.html)
  over Websockets. Start and stop kernels through REST calls.
  This HTTP API is compatible with the respective API sections
  of the Jupyter Notebook server.

- Serve HTTP requests from annotated notebook cells. The code snippets
  are cells of a static notebook configured in the Kernel Gateway.
  Annotations define which HTTP verbs and resources it supports.
  Incoming requests are served by executing one of the cells in a kernel.

Jupyter Kernel Gateway uses the same code as Jupyter Notebook
to launch kernels in its local process/filesystem space.
It can be containerized and scaled out using common technologies like [tmpnb](https://github.com/jupyter/tmpnb), [Cloud Foundry](https://github.com/cloudfoundry), and [Kubernetes](http://kubernetes.io/).

### Example Uses of Kernel Gateway

- Attach a local Jupyter Notebook server to a compute cluster in the cloud running near big data (e.g., interactive gateway to Spark)
- Enable a new breed of non-notebook web clients to provision and use kernels (e.g., web dashboards using [jupyter-js-services](https://github.com/jupyter/jupyter-js-services))
- Create microservices from notebooks using the Kernel Gateway [`notebook-http` mode](https://jupyter-kernel-gateway.readthedocs.io/en/latest/http-mode.html)

### Features

See the [Features page](https://jupyter-kernel-gateway.readthedocs.io/en/latest/features.html) in the
documentation for a list of the Jupyter Kernel Gateway features.

## Installation

Detailed installation instructions are located in the
[Getting Started page](https://jupyter-kernel-gateway.readthedocs.io/en/latest/getting-started.html)
of the project docs. Here's a quick start using `pip`:

```bash
# install from pypi
pip install jupyter_kernel_gateway

# show all config options
jupyter kernelgateway --help-all

# run it with default options
jupyter kernelgateway
```

## Contributing

The [Development page](https://jupyter-kernel-gateway.readthedocs.io/en/latest/devinstall.html) includes information about setting up a development environment and typical developer tasks.

modules.name: kernel_gateway.auth.identity
documentation:
Gateway Identity Provider interface

This defines the _authentication_ layer of Jupyter Server,
to be used in combination with Authorizer for _authorization_.
Copyright (c) Jupyter Development Team.
Distributed under the terms of the Modified BSD License.

classes.name: kernel_gateway.auth.identity.GatewayIdentityProvider

methods.name: kernel_gateway.auth.identity.GatewayIdentityProvider.should_check_origin
documentation:
Should the Handler check for CORS origin validation?

Origin check should be skipped for token-authenticated requests.

Returns:
- True, if Handler must check for valid CORS origin.
- False, if Handler should skip origin check since requests are token-authenticated.

methods.name: kernel_gateway.auth.identity.GatewayIdentityProvider.generate_anonymous_user
documentation:
Generate a random anonymous user.

For use when a single shared token is used,
but does not identify a user.

methods.name: kernel_gateway.auth.identity.GatewayIdentityProvider.is_token_authenticated
documentation:
The default authentication flow of Gateway is token auth.

The only other option is no auth
Interface for providing identity management and authentication for a Gateway server.

modules.name: kernel_gateway.mixins

Distributed under the terms of the Modified BSD License.

classes.name: kernel_gateway.mixins.CORSMixin

methods.name: kernel_gateway.mixins.CORSMixin.set_cors_headers
documentation:
Sets the CORS headers as the default for all responses.

Disables CSP configured by the notebook package. It's not necessary
for a programmatic API.

Notes
-----
This method name was changed from set_default_headers to set_cors_header
when adding support for JupyterServer 2.x.  In that release, JS changed the
way the headers were implemented due to changes in the way a user is authenticated.
See https://github.com/jupyter-server/jupyter_server/pull/671.

methods.name: kernel_gateway.mixins.CORSMixin.options
documentation:
Override the notebook implementation to return the headers
configured in `set_default_headers instead of the hardcoded set
supported by the handler base class in the notebook project.
Mixes CORS headers into tornado.web.RequestHandlers.

classes.name: kernel_gateway.mixins.TokenAuthorizationMixin

methods.name: kernel_gateway.mixins.TokenAuthorizationMixin.prepare
documentation:
Ensures the correct auth token is present, either as a parameter
`token=<value>` or as a header `Authorization: token <value>`.
Does nothing unless an auth token is configured in kg_auth_token.

If kg_auth_token is set and the token is not present, responds
with 401 Unauthorized.

Notes
-----
Implemented in prepare rather than in `get_user` to avoid interaction
with the `@web.authenticated` decorated methods in the notebook
package.

Mixes token auth into tornado.web.RequestHandlers and
tornado.websocket.WebsocketHandlers.

classes.name: kernel_gateway.mixins.JSONErrorsMixin

methods.name: kernel_gateway.mixins.JSONErrorsMixin.write_error
documentation:
Responds with an application/json error object.

Overrides the APIHandler.write_error in the notebook server until it
properly sets the 'reason' field.

Parameters
----------
status_code
    HTTP status code to set
**kwargs
    Arbitrary keyword args. Only uses `exc_info[1]`, if it exists,
    to get a `log_message`, `args`, and `reason` from a raised
    exception that triggered this method

Examples
--------
{"401", reason="Unauthorized", message="Invalid auth token"}

Mixes `write_error` into tornado.web.RequestHandlers to respond with
JSON format errors.

functions.name: kernel_gateway.notebook_http.request_utils.parse_body
documentation:
Parses the body of an HTTP request based on its Content-Type.

If no Content-Type is found, treats the value as plain text.

Parameters
----------
request : tornado.web.HTTPRequest
    Web request with the body to parse

Returns
-------
dict or str
    Dictionary of a form-encoded JSON-encoded body, raw string otherwise

functions.name: kernel_gateway.notebook_http.request_utils.parse_args
documentation:
Decodes UTF-8 encoded argument values.

Parameters
----------
args : dict

Returns
-------
dict
    Maps keys from args to decoded strings

functions.name: kernel_gateway.notebook_http.request_utils.headers_to_dict
documentation:
Turns a set of tornado headers into a Python dict.

Repeat headers are aggregated into lists.

Parameters
----------
headers : dict
    Key / value header pairs

Returns
-------
dict
    Maps keys from headers to values or lists of values

Copyright (c) Jupyter Development Team.
Distributed under the terms of the Modified BSD License.

functions.name: kernel_gateway.notebook_http.cell.parser.first_path_param_index
documentation:
Gets the index to the first path parameter for the endpoint. The
returned value is not the string index, but rather the depth of where the
endpoint is found in the the path.

# Parameters

endpoint

: URL

# Returns

int

: Path segment index value or sys.maxsize

# Examples

```pycon
>>> first_path_param_index("/foo/:bar")
1
>>> first_path_param_index("/foo/quo/:bar")
2
>>> first_path_param_index("/foo/quo/bar")
sys.maxsize
```

methods.name: kernel_gateway.notebook_http.cell.parser.APICellParser.is_api_cell

documentation:
Gets the list of all annotated ResponseInfo HTTP paths and verbs.

Returns

methods.name: kernel_gateway.notebook_http.cell.parser.APICellParser.get_default_api_spec
documentation:
Gets the default minimum API spec to use when building a full spec
from the seed notebook's contents.

dictionary
    Dictionary with a root "swagger" property

A utility class for parsing Jupyter code cells to find API annotations
of the form:

`COMMENT (ResponseInfo)? HTTP_VERB URL_PATH`

where:

* `COMMENT` is the single line comment character of the notebook kernel
    language
* `HTTP_VERB` is a valid HTTP verb
* `URL_PATH` is a valid HTTP URL path string with optional `:variable`
    placeholders
* `ResponseInfo` is a literal token.

Parameters
----------
comment_prefix
    Token indicating a comment in the notebook language

Attributes
----------
api_indicator : str
    Regex pattern for API annotations
api_response_indicator : str
    Regex pattern for API response metadata annotations

modules.name: kernel_gateway.notebook_http.swagger.parser

Pulls apart the first block comment of a cell's source,
then tries to parse it as a JSON object. If it contains a 'swagger'
property, returns it.

methods.name: kernel_gateway.notebook_http.swagger.parser.SwaggerCellParser._endpoint_verb_source_mappings
documentation:
Gets a dict of all paths, verbs, and contents for the given cells,
using the given regex to find the relevant operationIds.

Parameters
----------
source_cells
    List of source strings from notebook cells
operationIdRegex
    Regex for spotting a cell marked up with a match group that yields
    a Swagger operationId.

Returns
-------
dict
    Dict of dicts mapping the endpoint str to a HTTP verbs to concatenated
    cell sources

methods.name: kernel_gateway.notebook_http.swagger.parser.SwaggerCellParser.get_cell_endpoint_and_verb
documentation:
Gets the HTTP path and verb from an annotated cell.

If the cell is not annotated with an operationId, or the known Swagger
doc doesn't reference the same operationId, returns (None, None)

Parameters
----------
cell_source
    Source from a notebook cell

Returns
-------
tuple
    Endpoint str, HTTP verb str

methods.name: kernel_gateway.notebook_http.swagger.parser.SwaggerCellParser.get_path_content
documentation:
Gets the operation description for an API cell annotation.

Parameters
----------
cell_source
    Source from a notebook cell

Returns
-------
Object describing the supported operation. If the cell is not annotated,
just minimal response output guidance.

methods.name: kernel_gateway.notebook_http.swagger.parser.SwaggerCellParser.get_default_api_spec
documentation:
Gets the default minimum API spec to use when building a full spec
from the seed notebook's contents, preferably tak

dictionary
    Dictionary with a root "swagger" property

A utility class for parsing Jupyter code cells to find API annotations
 of the form:

 `COMMENT (ResponseInfo)? operationId: ID`

 where:

 * `COMMENT` is the single line comment character of the notebook kernel
     language
 * `ResponseInfo` is a literal token.
 * `ID` is an operation's ID as documented in a Swagger comment block
 * `operationId` is a literal token.

 Parameters
 ----------
 comment_prefix
     Token indicating a comment in the notebook language

 Attributes
 ----------
notebook_cells : list
     The cells from the target notebook, one of which must contain a Swagger spec in a commented block
 operation_indicator : str
     Regex pattern for API annotations
 operation_response_indicator : str
     Regex pattern for API response metadata annotations

methods.name: kernel_gateway.gatewayapp.KernelGatewayApp._load_notebook
documentation:
Loads a notebook from the local filesystem or HTTP(S) URL.

Raises
------
RuntimeError if there is no kernel spec matching the one specified in
the notebook or forced via configuration.

Returns
-------
object
    Notebook object from nbformat

methods.name: kernel_gateway.gatewayapp.KernelGatewayApp.initialize
documentation:
Initializes the base class, configurable manager instances, the
Tornado web app, and the tornado HTTP server.

Parameters
----------
argv
    Command line arguments

new_httpserver
    Indicates that a new HTTP server instance should be created

methods.name: kernel_gateway.gatewayapp.KernelGatewayApp.init_configurables
documentation:
Initializes all configurable objects including a kernel manager, kernel
spec manager, session manager, and personality.

Any kernel pool configured by the personality will be its responsibility
to shut down.

Optionally, loads a notebook and prespawns the configured number of
kernels.

methods.name: kernel_gateway.gatewayapp.KernelGatewayApp.init_webapp
documentation:
Initializes Tornado web application with uri handlers.

Adds the various managers and web-front configuration values to the
Tornado settings for reference by the handlers.

methods.name: kernel_gateway.gatewayapp.KernelGatewayApp._build_ssl_options
documentation:
Build a dictionary of SSL options for the tornado HTTP server.

Taken directly from jupyter/notebook code.

methods.name: kernel_gateway.gatewayapp.KernelGatewayApp.init_http_server
documentation:
Initializes a HTTP server for the Tornado web application on the
configured interface and port.

Tries to find an open port if the one configured is not available using
the same logic as the Jupyer Notebook server.

methods.name: kernel_gateway.gatewayapp.KernelGatewayApp.init_io_loop
documentation: init self.io_loop so that an extension can use it by io_loop.call_later() to create background tasks

methods.name: kernel_gateway.gatewayapp.KernelGatewayApp.init_signal
documentation: Initialize signal handlers.

methods.name: kernel_gateway.gatewayapp.KernelGatewayApp._handle_sigint
documentation: SIGINT handler spawns confirmation dialog

methods.name: kernel_gateway.gatewayapp.KernelGatewayApp._restore_sigint_handler
documentation: callback for restoring original SIGINT handler

methods.name: kernel_gateway.gatewayapp.KernelGatewayApp._confirm_exit
documentation:
confirm shutdown on ^C

A second ^C, or answering 'y' within 5s will cause shutdown,
otherwise original SIGINT handler will be restored.

This doesn't work on Windows.

methods.name: kernel_gateway.gatewayapp.KernelGatewayApp._signal_stop
documentation: Handle a stop signal.

methods.name: kernel_gateway.gatewayapp.KernelGatewayApp.start_app
documentation: Starts the application (with ioloop to follow).

methods.name: kernel_gateway.gatewayapp.KernelGatewayApp.start
documentation: Starts an IO loop for the application.

methods.name: kernel_gateway.gatewayapp.KernelGatewayApp._stop
documentation: Cleanup resources and stop the IO Loop.

methods.name: kernel_gateway.gatewayapp.KernelGatewayApp.stop
documentation: Cleanup resources and stop the server.

methods.name: kernel_gateway.gatewayapp.KernelGatewayApp.shutdown
documentation: Stop all kernels in the pool.

methods.name: kernel_gateway.gatewayapp.KernelGatewayApp.async_shutdown
documentation: Stop all kernels in the pool.

class_variables.name: kernel_gateway.gatewayapp.KernelGatewayApp.auth_token_env
documentation: noqa: S105

class_variables.name: kernel_gateway.gatewayapp.KernelGatewayApp._log_formatter_cls
documentation: traitlet default is LevelFormatter

Application that provisions Jupyter kernels and proxies HTTP/Websocket
traffic to the kernels.

- reads command line and environment variable settings
- initializes managers and routes
- creates a Tornado HTTP server
- starts the Tornado event loop

modules.name: kernel_gateway.services.sessions.sessionmanager
documentation: Session manager that keeps all its metadata in memory.
Copyright (c) Jupyter Development Team.
Distributed under the terms of the Modified BSD License.

classes.name: kernel_gateway.services.sessions.sessionmanager.SessionManager

methods.name: kernel_gateway.services.sessions.sessionmanager.SessionManager.session_exists
documentation:
Checks to see if the session with the given path value exists.

Parameters
----------
path : str
    Session path value to search on

Returns
-------
bool

methods.name: kernel_gateway.services.sessions.sessionmanager.SessionManager.new_session_id
documentation: Creates a uuid for a new session.

methods.name: kernel_gateway.services.sessions.sessionmanager.SessionManager.create_session
documentation:
Creates a session and returns its model.

Launches a kernel and stores the session metadata for later lookup.

Parameters
----------
path : str
    Path value to store in the session metadata
kernel_name : str
    Kernel spec name
kernel_id : str
    Existing kernel ID to bind to the session (unsupported)

Returns
-------
dict
    Session model

methods.name: kernel_gateway.services.sessions.sessionmanager.SessionManager.save_session
documentation:
Saves the metadata for the session with the given `session_id`.

Given a `session_id` (and any other of the arguments), this method
appends a dictionary to the in-memory list of sessions.

Parameters
----------
session_id : str
    UUID for the session; this method must be given a session_id
path : str
    Path for the given notebook
kernel_id : str
    ID for the kernel associated with this session

Returns
-------
dict
    Session model with `session_id`, `path`, and `kernel_id` keys

methods.name: kernel_gateway.services.sessions.sessionmanager.SessionManager.get_session_by_key
documentation:
Gets the first session with the given key/value pair.

Parameters
----------
key : hashable
    Session metadata key to match
value : any
    Session metadata value to match

Returns
-------
dict
    Matching session model or None if not found

methods.name: kernel_gateway.services.sessions.sessionmanager.SessionManager.get_session
documentation:
Returns the model for a particular session.

Takes a keyword argument and searches for the value in the in-memory
session store. Returns the entire session model.

Parameters
----------
**kwargs : keyword argument
    One of the key/value pairs from `_columns`

Raises
------
TypeError
    If there are no kwargs or none of them match a key/column used in
    the metadata
tornado.web.HTTPError
    404 Not Found if no session matches the provided metadata

Returns
-------
model : dict
    All the information from the session described by the kwarg

methods.name: kernel_gateway.services.sessions.sessionmanager.SessionManager.update_session
documentation:
Updates the values in the session store.

Update the values of the session model with the given `session_id`
with the values from the keyword arguments.

Parameters
----------
session_id : str
    UUID that identifies a session in the sqlite3 database
**kwargs : str
    Key/value pairs to store

Raises
------
KeyError
    If no session matches the given `session_id`

methods.name: kernel_gateway.services.sessions.sessionmanager.SessionManager.row_to_model
documentation:
Turns a "row" in the in-memory session store into a model dictionary.

Parameters
----------
row : dict
    Maps `id` to `session_id`, `notebook` to a dict containing the
    `path`, and `kernel` to the kernel model looked up using the
    `kernel_id`

methods.name: kernel_gateway.services.sessions.sessionmanager.SessionManager.list_sessions
documentation:
Returns a list of dictionaries containing all the information from
the session store.

Returns
-------
list
    Dictionaries from `row_to_model`

methods.name: kernel_gateway.services.sessions.sessionmanager.SessionManager.delete_session
documentation:
Deletes the session in the session store with given `session_id`.

Raises
------
KeyError
    If the `session_id` is not in the store

Simple implementation of the SessionManager interface that allows clients
to associate basic metadata with a kernel.

Parameters
----------
kernel_manager : SeedingMappingKernelManager
    Used to start a kernel when creating a session

Attributes
----------
kernel_manager : SeedingMappingKernelManager
    Used to start a kernel when creating a session
_sessions : list
    Sessions
_columns : list
    Session metadata key names

modules.name: kernel_gateway.notebook_http.handlers
documentation: Tornado handlers for invoking notebook cells as web APIs.
Copyright (c) Jupyter Development Team.
Distributed under the terms of the Modified BSD License.

classes.name: kernel_gateway.notebook_http.handlers.NotebookAPIHandler

methods.name: kernel_gateway.notebook_http.handlers.NotebookAPIHandler.finish_future
documentation:
Resolves the promise to respond to a HTTP request handled by a
kernel in the pool.

Defines precedence for the kind of response:

1. If any error occurred, resolve with a CodeExecutionError exception
2. If any stream message was collected from the kernel, resolve with
    the joined string of stream messages
3. If an execute_result was collected, resolve with that result object
    JSON encoded
4. Resolve with an empty string

Parameters
----------
future : tornado.concurrent.Future
    Promise of a future response to an API request
result_accumulator : dict
    Dictionary of results from a kernel with at least the keys error,
    stream, and result

methods.name: kernel_gateway.notebook_http.handlers.NotebookAPIHandler.on_recv
documentation:
Collects ipoub messages associated with code execution request
identified by `parent_header`.

Continues collecting until an execution state of idle is reached.
The first three parameters are typically applied in a partial.

Parameters
----------
result_accumulator : dict
    Accumulates data from `execute_result`, `stream` and `error`
    messages under keys `result`, `stream`, and `error` respectively
    across multiple invocations of this callback.
future : tornado.concurrent.Future
    Promise to resolve when the kernel goes idle
parent_header : dict
    Parent header from an `execute` request, used to identify messages
    that relate to its execution vs other executions
msg : dict
    Kernel message received from the iopub channel

methods.name: kernel_gateway.notebook_http.handlers.NotebookAPIHandler.execute_code
documentation:
Executes `source_code` on the kernel specified.

Registers a callback for iopub messages. Promises to return the output
of the execution in the future after the kernel returns to its idle
state.

Parameters
----------
kernel_client : object
    Client to use to execute the code
kernel_id : str
    ID of the kernel from the pool that will execute the request
source_code : str
    Source code to execute

Returns
-------
tornado.concurrent.Future
    Promise of execution result

Raises
------
CodeExecutionError
    If the kernel returns any error

methods.name: kernel_gateway.notebook_http.handlers.NotebookAPIHandler._handle_request
documentation:
Turns an HTTP request into annotated notebook code to execute on a
kernel.

Sets the HTTP response code, headers, and response body based on the
result of the kernel execution. Then finishes the Tornado response.

Executes code from a notebook cell in response to HTTP requests at the
route registered in association with this class.

Supports the GET, POST, PUT, and DELETE HTTP methods.

Parameters
----------
sources : dict
    Maps HTTP verb strings to annotated cells extracted from a notebook
response_sources
    Maps HTTP verb strings to ResponseInfo annotated cells extracted from a
    notebook
kernel_pool
    Instance of services.kernels.ManagedKernelPool
kernel_name
    Kernel spec name used to launch the kernel pool
kernel_language
    Kernel spec language used to make language specific operations

Attributes
----------
See parameters: they are stored as passed as instance variables.

See Also
--------
services.cell.parser.APICellParser for detail about how the source cells
are identified, parsed, and associated with HTTP verbs and paths.

classes.name: kernel_gateway.notebook_http.handlers.NotebookDownloadHandler
documentation: Handles requests to download the annotated notebook behind the web API.

modules.name: kernel_gateway.services.kernelspecs.handlers
documentation: Tornado handlers for kernel specs.
Copyright (c) Jupyter Development Team.
Distributed under the terms of the Modified BSD License.

modules.name: kernel_gateway.__main__
documentation: CLI entrypoint for the kernel gateway package.
Copyright (c) Jupyter Development Team.
Distributed under the terms of the Modified BSD License.

modules.name: kernel_gateway.services.kernels.manager
documentation: Kernel manager that optionally seeds kernel memory.
Copyright (c) Jupyter Development Team.
Distributed under the terms of the Modified BSD License.

classes.name: kernel_gateway.services.kernels.manager.SeedingMappingKernelManager

methods.name: kernel_gateway.services.kernels.manager.SeedingMappingKernelManager.seed_kernelspec
documentation:
Gets the kernel spec name for run the seed notebook.

Prefers the spec name forced by configuration over the spec in the
seed notebook itself.

Returns
-------
str
    Name of the notebook kernelspec or None if no seed notebook exists

methods.name: kernel_gateway.services.kernels.manager.SeedingMappingKernelManager.seed_source
documentation:
Gets the source of the seed notebook in cell order.

Returns
-------
list
    Notebook code cell contents or None if no seed notebook exists

methods.name: kernel_gateway.services.kernels.manager.SeedingMappingKernelManager.start_seeded_kernel
documentation:
Start a kernel using the language specified in the seed notebook.

Run synchronously so that any exceptions thrown while seed rise up
to the caller.

methods.name: kernel_gateway.services.kernels.manager.SeedingMappingKernelManager.start_kernel
documentation:
Starts a kernel and then executes a list of code cells on it if a
seed notebook exists.

Extends the server's kernel manager to optionally execute the contents
of a notebook on a kernel when it starts.

classes.name: kernel_gateway.services.kernels.manager.KernelGatewayIOLoopKernelManager
documentation:
Extends the IOLoopKernelManager used by the SeedingMappingKernelManager.

Sets the environment variable 'KERNEL_GATEWAY' to '1' to indicate that the
kernel is executing within a Jupyter Kernel Gateway instance. Removes the
KG_AUTH_TOKEN from the environment variables passed to the kernel when it
starts.

modules.name: kernel_gateway.services.kernels.handlers
documentation: Tornado handlers for kernel CRUD and communication.
Copyright (c) Jupyter Development Team.
Distributed under the terms of the Modified BSD License.

classes.name: kernel_gateway.services.kernels.handlers.MainKernelHandler

methods.name: kernel_gateway.services.kernels.handlers.MainKernelHandler.post
documentation:
Overrides the super class method to honor the max number of allowed
kernels configuration setting and to support custom kernel environment
variables for every request.

Delegates the request to the super class implementation if no limit is
set or if the maximum is not reached. Otherwise, responds with an
error.

Raises
------
tornado.web.HTTPError
    403 Forbidden if the limit is reached

methods.name: kernel_gateway.services.kernels.handlers.MainKernelHandler.get
documentation:
Overrides the super class method to honor the kernel listing
configuration setting.

Allows the request to reach the super class if listing is enabled.

Raises
------
tornado.web.HTTPError
    403 Forbidden if kernel listing is disabled

methods.name: kernel_gateway.services.kernels.handlers.MainKernelHandler.options
documentation: Method for properly handling CORS pre-flight

Extends the notebook main kernel handler with token auth, CORS, and
JSON errors.

classes.name: kernel_gateway.services.kernels.handlers.KernelHandler

methods.name: kernel_gateway.services.kernels.handlers.KernelHandler.options
documentation: Method for properly handling CORS pre-flight

Extends the notebook kernel handler with token auth, CORS, and
JSON errors.

modules.name: kernel_gateway.services.kernels.pool
documentation: Kernel pools that track and delegate to kernels.
Copyright (c) Jupyter Development Team.
Distributed under the terms of the Modified BSD License.

classes.name: kernel_gateway.services.kernels.pool.KernelPool

methods.name: kernel_gateway.services.kernels.pool.KernelPool.shutdown
documentation: Shuts down all running kernels.

Spawns a pool of kernels.

The kernel pool is responsible for clean up and shutdown of individual
kernels that are members of the pool.

Parameters
----------
prespawn_count
    Number of kernels to spawn immediately
kernel_manager
    Kernel manager instance

classes.name: kernel_gateway.services.kernels.pool.ManagedKernelPool

methods.name: kernel_gateway.services.kernels.pool.ManagedKernelPool.acquire
documentation:
Gets a kernel client and removes it from the available pool of
clients.

Returns
-------
tuple
    Kernel client instance, kernel ID

methods.name: kernel_gateway.services.kernels.pool.ManagedKernelPool.release
documentation:
Puts a kernel back into the pool of kernels available to handle
requests.

Parameters
----------
kernel_id : str
    Kernel to return to the pool

methods.name: kernel_gateway.services.kernels.pool.ManagedKernelPool._on_reply
documentation:
Invokes the iopub callback registered for the `kernel_id` and passes
it a deserialized list of kernel messages.

Parameters
----------
kernel_id : str
    Kernel that sent the reply
msg_list : list
    List of 0mq messages

methods.name: kernel_gateway.services.kernels.pool.ManagedKernelPool.create_on_reply
documentation:
Creates an anonymous function to handle reply messages from the
kernel.

Parameters
----------
kernel_id
    Kernel to listen to

Returns
-------
function
    Callback function taking a kernel ID and 0mq message list

methods.name: kernel_gateway.services.kernels.pool.ManagedKernelPool.on_recv
documentation:
Registers a callback function for iopub messages from a particular
kernel.

This is needed to avoid having multiple callbacks per kernel client.

Parameters
----------
kernel_id
    Kernel from which to receive iopub messages
func
    Callback function to use for kernel iopub messages

methods.name: kernel_gateway.services.kernels.pool.ManagedKernelPool.shutdown
documentation: Shuts down all kernels and their clients.

Spawns a pool of kernels that are treated as identical delegates for
future requests.

Manages access to individual kernels using a borrower/lender pattern.
Cleans them all up when shut down.

Parameters
----------
prespawn_count
    Number of kernels to spawn immediately
kernel_manager
    Kernel manager instance

Attributes
----------
kernel_clients : dict
    Map of kernel IDs to client instances for communicating with them
on_recv_funcs : dict
    Map of kernel IDs to iopub callback functions
kernel_pool : list
    List of available delegate kernel IDs
kernel_semaphore : tornado.locks.Semaphore
    Semaphore that controls access to the kernel pool
